# Overall strucutere
```
1. Numeric responses with categorical predictors enter the central-tendency branch detailed in section
\ref{sec:numeric-categoric}.
2a) Ordered categorical responses with categorical predictors follow the non-parametric Wilcoxon or Kruskal--Wallis route; 
2b  when both variables are ordered and `correlation = TRUE`, Kendall rank correlation is used.

4a) Two numeric variables enter simple linear regression 
4b) `correlation = TRUE`, which switches to Spearman rank correlation.
5. Two unordered factors enter the proportion-comparison branch.




# Example structure 


Route 1  Numeric response, categorical predictor

 k=2 Students t-test, general case k>2 Fisher Anova
 k=2 Welch t-test-,  general case k>2Welch Anova
k=2  Wilcoxn rank-> General casek>2 Kruskal Wallis 


Route 2  Ordered response 
a)  ordered reponse, categorical factor k=2  Wilcoxn rank-> k>2 Kruskal Wallis 
b) ordered reponse, ordered factor 
k=2  Wilcoxn rank-> k>2 Kruskal Wallis -> show parallel, or avoid as doubling???
b+ correlation =TRUE Kendall
Route 3 Numeric response, numeric predictor 
a) Linear regression 
b) with correlation=True Spearman Corrleation 
Route4  Two unordered factors
Chi2, Fisher
